criminal_and_law_enforcement_record.open_source_review_conclusion: No records found in open-source review. Clean criminal record as of research date.

prior_quotes_in_press.paraphrase: Quoted regarding the Coleman superintendent saga at Harleton ISD. Specific quote and context require further research.

about_public_record.about_summary_narrative:
Patrick McGill is the longest-serving current member of the Harleton ISD Board of Trustees, holding Place 6 since November 2016. He formerly served as Board Vice President.

McGill has been reelected twice: in 2020 and most recently in November 2024, when he defeated challenger Shaun Borden. His current term runs through 2028, and he is NOT on the November 2026 ballot.

McGill was quoted in the Longview News-Journal during the 2018 Coleman superintendent saga, indicating he was an active voice on the board during that period of district turmoil. The details of his statements and the full context of the Coleman situation require further research.

No negative public-record items have surfaced in open-source review as of this research date.

summary: Patrick McGill holds Place 6 on the Harleton ISD Board. Longest-serving current trustee (since Nov 2016). Reelected 2020 and 2024 (beat Shaun Borden). Former Board Vice President. Quoted in the News-Journal regarding the 2018 Coleman superintendent saga. NOT on the 2026 ballot (term through 2028). Clean criminal record in open-source review.
analyst_notes: McGill is the institutional memory of this board, having served through the 2018 Coleman superintendent crisis, the 2018 bond failure, and the 2024 turnover cycle. His 2024 contested race against Shaun Borden suggests he is not universally popular despite his tenure. As the longest-serving member, he likely wields significant informal influence. Lower priority for deep research since he is not on the 2026 ballot, but his 2018 News-Journal quotes regarding the Coleman saga should be pulled for context on district governance history.
